title: v2: healthcheck not running as root
body:
This works as rootless, but not as root:
```console
# podman run -d --name foo --health-cmd /bin/false --health-interval 1s --health-retries 3 alpine sleep 999
e544ff5f0c0ff74e479cc220f1d01ad79be8ae0afb47f2606cd8225011cd407d
# podman inspect --format '{{.State.Healthcheck}}' foo
{starting 0 []}
```
No matter how long I wait (up to a few minutes, anyway) the healthcheck status never changes. On rootless, though:
```
$ podman inspect --format '{{.State.Healthcheck}}' foo                                                                                             bats
{unhealthy 54 [{2020-06-03T15:59:30.055797859-06:00 2020-06-03T15:59:30.091628723-06:00 1 } {2020-06-03T15:59:34.060611071-06:00 2020-06-03T15:59:34.094876023-06:00 1 } {2020-06-03T15:59:36.048225043-06:00 2020-06-03T15:59:36.086964819-06:00 1 } {2020-06-03T15:59:38.048209412-06:00 2020-06-03T15:59:38.106115312-06:00 1 } {2020-06-03T15:59:40.085815259-06:00 2020-06-03T15:59:40.117754993-06:00 1 }]}
```
Also works fine with podman-1.9.3-1.fc32

This is master @ 377554d681db749547100d4cead47fb42fd9c414

No need to write a test when you fix this - that's the whole point of this PR, I have a BATS test in the works that caught this.

comments.body: Oh, never mind, looks like it's an AVC. Setting context on `bin/podman` fixes it. Sorry.
